user_query:
I'm drafting a column on how corporate influence chips away at the spirit of fan loyalty, and I want to ground it in real voices. Using the local files in this workspace, please: 1) extract all fan comments from input/fan_comments.html, 2) translate any non-English comments to plain, neutral English (preserve meaning without editorializing), 3) filter to only teams listed in input/teams.json (field: target_teams), 4) enrich each comment with the team's primary sponsor from input/sponsors.csv, and 5) select and rank the comments based on the number of corporate-influence keywords they contain. Save two outputs: a) output/quotes_ranked.csv and b) output/top5_excerpts.md.

Details and constraints:
- Inputs: input/fan_comments.html (HTML with comment records), input/sponsors.csv (CSV with columns: team,sponsor), input/teams.json (JSON with key: target_teams).
- Extract from HTML: for each <div class="comment"> record, capture data-id, data-team, data-language, and its text content.
- Translation: translate to English only if data-language != "en". Maintain names and proper nouns (e.g., team and sponsor) unchanged.
- Filtering: keep only comments whose data-team appears in input/teams.json target_teams AND that contain at least one keyword from the list below when checked against either the original or translated text (case-insensitive substring match).
- Keywords (bilingual; match against either version of the text):
  English: ["sponsor", "naming rights", "betting", "ticket prices", "TV deal", "paywall", "owner", "corporate", "brand", "stadium rebrand", "merch prices"]
  Spanish: ["patrocinador", "derechos de nombre", "apuestas", "precios de entradas", "acuerdo de TV", "muro de pago", "propietario", "corporativo", "marca", "rebrand del estadio", "precios de mercancía"]
- Ranking: relevance_score = count of unique matched keywords (from both lists) found in the original or translated quote. Sort descending by relevance_score; break ties by translated_quote length (characters) descending.
- Output a): output/quotes_ranked.csv must include header and records with columns: id,team,sponsor,original_language,original_quote,translated_quote,relevance_score,keywords_found (semicolon-separated keywords actually matched). Records must be sorted per the ranking rule.
- Output b): output/top5_excerpts.md should include exactly the top 5 ranked comments. For each, include three lines: "Team — Sponsor: <team> — <sponsor>", "Quote: <translated_quote>", and "Why it resonates: <one concise sentence referencing at least one matched keyword>". Separate entries with a blank line.
- Cross-checks: every id in outputs must appear in input/fan_comments.html; every team-sponsor pairing must match input/sponsors.csv; teams must be limited to input/teams.json.

Input files are:
- input/fan_comments.html
- input/sponsors.csv
- input/teams.json

Please produce the two outputs at the specified paths so I can cite, rank, and excerpt the most resonant fan voices in my piece. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
